name: google-analytics-automation
description: Automate Google Analytics tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): run reports, list accounts/properties, funnels, pivots, key events. Always search tools first for current schemas.
capabilities: List GA4 accounts and properties via API
Run standard and batch reports with custom dimensions and metrics
Execute pivot reports for cross-tabulated data analysis
Run funnel reports to analyze conversion drop-off rates
Manage key events (conversions) in GA4 properties
Discover available dimensions and metrics via metadata API
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The 92 'external_commands' detections are MCP tool names (e.g., GOOGLE_ANALYTICS_LIST_ACCOUNTS), not shell executions. The hardcoded URL is a legitimate MCP server endpoint. No cryptographic algorithms or system reconnaissance present. This is a legitimate Google Analytics automation skill using Rube MCP.
valueStatement: Manually pulling GA4 reports is time-consuming. This skill automates Google Analytics reporting through Rube MCP, letting you run standard reports, batch reports, pivot tables, and funnel analysis with simple commands.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server connection with Google Analytics toolkit
User must complete Google OAuth authentication
Batch reports limited to 5 reports per call
Cannot create new GA4 properties or accounts

useCases.title: Weekly Marketing Reports
useCases.description: Automate weekly traffic and conversion reports by running standard GA4 reports with date ranges and key metrics like sessions, users, and conversions.

useCases.title: Conversion Funnel Analysis
useCases.description: Analyze user conversion paths using funnel reports to identify drop-off points and optimize marketing funnels.

useCases.title: Multi-Property Dashboard
useCases.description: Query multiple GA4 properties in batch to aggregate data across properties for executive dashboards.

promptTemplates.title: List Available Properties
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the google-analytics-automation skill to list all available GA4 properties for my account.
promptTemplates.scenario: Quickly discover which GA4 properties are accessible

promptTemplates.title: Run Traffic Report
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the google-analytics-automation skill to run a GA4 report for the last 30 days showing sessions, users, and bounce rate by device category.

promptTemplates.title: Analyze Conversion Funnel
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the google-analytics-automation skill to run a funnel report for the checkout flow. Steps: page_view (home), add_to_cart, begin_checkout, purchase. Show conversion rates between each step.

promptTemplates.title: Batch Executive Report
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the google-analytics-automation skill to run a batch report with: (1) traffic sources summary, (2) top pages by views, (3) key events count. Property: [YOUR_PROPERTY_ID], Date range: last 7 days.

bestPractices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before making API calls
Use GET_METADATA to discover valid dimensions and metrics before running reports
Verify dimension/metric compatibility with CHECK_COMPATIBILITY to avoid report errors
antiPatterns: Using numeric property IDs instead of full resource names (e.g., use 'properties/123456' not '123456')
Assuming today's data is complete - GA4 has processing delays
Running reports without checking if the Google Analytics connection is ACTIVE

faq.question: What is Rube MCP?
faq.answer: Rube MCP is a Model Context Protocol server that provides access to various toolkits including Google Analytics. It handles OAuth authentication with Google.

faq.question: Do I need a Google API key?
faq.answer: No. Rube MCP handles authentication via OAuth. You only need to add the MCP server endpoint and complete Google OAuth when prompted.

faq.question: How do I find my property ID?
faq.answer: Use GOOGLE_ANALYTICS_LIST_PROPERTIES to get a list of all properties. Property IDs are in format 'properties/123456'.

faq.question: What is the difference between standard, batch, and pivot reports?
faq.answer: Standard reports return simple metric/dimension tables. Batch reports run multiple reports in one call. Pivot reports create cross-tabulated data like Excel pivot tables.

faq.question: Why did my report return no data?
faq.answer: Check that your property ID is correct, date range has data, and dimensions/metrics are compatible. Also verify the connection status is ACTIVE.

faq.question: Can I create new GA4 properties with this skill?
faq.answer: No. This skill only reads data and lists existing properties. Property creation must be done in the Google Analytics UI or Admin API.
